audit_items.proof_trace: All three functions in debug.c are safe from null pointer dereference: lutil_debug_file never dereferences its file parameter within the file; lutil_debug's fmt argument is always a string literal from the Log()/Debug() macros (attackers control variadic args, not the format pointer), and ber_pvt_log_print is initialized non-null and guarded against null assignment by ber_set_option's invalue null check (options.c:131); eb_syslog is EBCDIC-only and uses the same safe patterns. The proof holds across all code paths.
